description: Create Kiro Spec and Steering files for Human-in-the-Room Directives feature under .kiro/ directory
steps: Create .kiro/steering/product-principles.md with the principle: 'RepoScope is a shared engineering room, not a chatbot. Humans and AI engineers collaborate through shared structured project state. Human directives are team context and must influence subsequent agent work in an auditable way.'

Create .kiro/specs/human-in-the-room/requirements.md with explicit acceptance criteria covering: directive creation, event persistence, state projection, context propagation, team room UI, auditability, and privacy/security

Create .kiro/specs/human-in-the-room/design.md documenting the technical design: new 'human-directive' event type, HumanDirective interface, state projector extension, context assembler integration, prompt builder formatting, API route, and UI components
Create .kiro/specs/human-in-the-room/tasks.md with implementation tasks broken down into logical groups
verification: Verify all four files exist with non-empty, well-structured markdown content
findings: Had to remove .kiro/ from .gitignore to allow spec files to be tracked in git. All four files created with well-structured markdown. Design references the existing 13 event types and documents adding a 14th (human-directive). Requirements cover all 8 acceptance criteria areas. Tasks are broken into 7 groups with dependency ordering.
